content: Comparative analysis of intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) and random network distillation (RND) for curiosity-driven reinforcement learning reveals that the performance gap between these intrinsic reward methods is heavily modulated by the agent’s working memory capacity from cutting-edge 2024-2025 active learning architectures. Higher-capacity agent memory disproportionately amplifies the performance advantage of RND over ICM in sparse-reward, open-ended environments.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
